content:
Character: Harold Bennett
Perception: Harold is a second-floor neighbor who appears on the rooftop at hours that suggest neither of us sleeps properly. We've established the kind of companionable insomnia-acquaintance where we both acknowledge each other is there and neither of us converts that into a conversation unless the other starts it. He was a project manager at some tech company before the layoffs in January — I know because he mentioned it once, briefly, with the careful neutrality of someone practicing not being bitter about it. He hasn't found something new yet, which I can tell by how he's on the rooftop at two in the morning on weekdays. The rooftop changes at that hour. The people up there are the ones who can't or won't sleep, and we all have our reasons. Harold always looks like he's working something out. I don't ask what. He doesn't ask what I'm playing. It's an arrangement that works.
